core_idea: Six formulas. Six colors. Six archetypes. One ritual.

images.title: Hero Six-Can Pack
images.subtitle: Six-pack slim cans in a premium tray
images.caption: The clearest retail hero for the color-coded formula system: six slim cans presented as one premium ritual set, with each formula visually differentiated through color, number, English name, Chinese character, and guardian artwork.
images.recommendation: Use this as the lead DTC and specialty retail SKU when the objective is trial, gifting, and fast brand comprehension.

images.title: Premium Single-Can Lineup
images.subtitle: Bold color-coded team image
images.caption: The strongest brand-recognition image: six individual cans stand side by side, making the formula architecture instantly readable while preserving a dramatic, premium wellness feel.
images.recommendation: Use across landing-page hero sections, launch ads, retail sell sheets, and investor materials.

images.title: Powder Refill Pouches
images.subtitle: Sustainable, collectible refill architecture
images.caption: The pouch system translates the six formulas into a lower-waste, repeat-purchase format while keeping the dramatic guardian-panel identity intact.
images.recommendation: Position as the eco-friendly subscription product after the customer has learned the system through cans or elixir bottles.

images.title: Reusable Powder Tin + Refill System
images.subtitle: Refillable tin, pouch, scoop, powder
images.caption: A reusable canister anchors the system as a long-term ritual rather than a disposable novelty. Formula panels around the tin create a premium shelf object with collectible energy.
images.recommendation: Make this the best repeat-purchase economics play: tin as the durable starter item, pouches as the recurring replenishment.

images.title: Tincture Dropper Set
images.subtitle: Premium botanical apothecary collection
images.caption: Amber glass bottles and dropper caps make the system feel more serious and apothecary-led, while the color-coded labels keep the heroic formula architecture recognizable.
images.recommendation: Use as a premium extension for customers who want concentrated botanical support and a more adult ritual format.

images.title: Liquid Elixir Bottle Set
images.subtitle: Six small daily elixir bottles in a tray
images.caption: Small amber elixir bottles create a daily-course product that is more direct and giftable than tinctures, with character cards reinforcing the six-archetype ritual.
images.recommendation: A strong premium starter kit candidate: easier to explain than tinctures and more giftable than bulk powder.

images.title: Soft Chews / Gummies Bulk Jar
images.subtitle: Lower-waste mass wellness extension
images.caption: The amber jar and weekly tray bring the system into a more familiar supplement format while the guardian color strip prevents the product from becoming generic wellness packaging.
images.recommendation: Use later as a mass wellness extension once the SERES SIX ritual language is established.

images.title: Full Product Family Hero Shot
images.subtitle: Complete brand world: cans, tins, pouches, bottles, chews
images.caption: This frame demonstrates how the visual system scales across formats without losing cohesion: six colors, six guardians, one ritual language.
images.recommendation: Use for brand-launch presentations, category strategy, investor decks, and packaging alignment meetings.

images.title: Retail Shelf / DTC Kit Shot
images.subtitle: Commercially believable launch bundle
images.caption: A cleaner retail and direct-to-consumer kit view shows how the system can live in a premium wellness environment without feeling childish or overly clinical.
images.recommendation: Use for pitch materials focused on channel strategy, merchandising, and online kit configuration.

images.title: Close-Up Detail Image
images.subtitle: Foil, embossing, character art, metallic surfaces
images.caption: The detail study shows the tactile premium cues needed to move the concept beyond flat character packaging: metallic ink, embossed logo treatment, black stock, brush accents, and reusable-tin details.
images.recommendation: Use this as a production briefing image for print vendors, packaging designers, and prototype teams.
